name: unit test refactor
overview: Normalize Jvedio.Test unit-test structure, naming, isolation, and maintenance rules without expanding into broad production refactors.
content: Refactor unit-test structure inside Jvedio.Test only. Keep production code changes out of scope unless absolutely necessary for test compilation.

todolist.content: Review current UnitTests and identify legacy, floating, or inconsistent test files
Define what should remain unit test versus integration test within Jvedio.Test
Plan directory, naming, bootstrap, and helper cleanup for Jvedio.Test
Prepare a minimal execution handoff for build mode after user approval
Redesign scan test config to use only input root, output root, report settings, and minimal switches
Implement scan test flow that reads files from input root, organizes matched videos, leaves unmatched files in place, and emits a summary report
Update the scan PowerShell entry and validation rules to use the simplified config and output report

optionPaths.name: Minimal test-only refactor
optionPaths.applicability: Use when the goal is to improve Jvedio.Test structure without broad production impact.
costs: Some deep testability problems remain for later
risks: A few awkward reflection or global-state patterns may remain temporarily
optionPaths.impact: Execution focuses on Jvedio.Test structure, csproj cleanup, helper normalization, and documentation sync.

optionPaths.name: Test refactor plus production seam cleanup
optionPaths.applicability: Use when small production-code changes are allowed to improve testability now.
risks: Feature scope may expand beyond test refactor
optionPaths.impact: Would require touching production classes and validation beyond Jvedio.Test.

resolvedFindings: Legacy `ScanTest/ImportTest.cs` is already absent; scan import coverage now lives in `UnitTests/Core/Scan/ScanTaskImportTests.cs` and `IntegrationTests/Scan/ScanImportIntegrationTests.cs`.
Previously tracked `UnitTests/Core/Crawler/CrawlerServer.cs` is also absent and not referenced by `Jvedio.Test.csproj`.
reviewContext.nextPlanningFocus: Feature completed. Keep this plan as the finished reference until the next active feature replaces it.
resolvedByUser: User accepted path A to remove residual empty-directory and stale-doc drift in the same build pass.
User chose a simplified scan-test configuration driven by input/output roots and minimal switches, with lookup-and-organize-only behavior.
completionSummary: The unit-test refactor work has passed functional verification.
The simplified scan test workflow, documentation sync, and test-structure cleanup are now treated as completed for this feature.
